records.ety: [Cf. Sw. flundra; akin to Dan. flynder, Icel. fly<?/ra, G. flunder, and perh. to E. flounder, v.i.]

defs: A flatfish of the family Pleuronectidæ, of many species.
notes: ☞ The common English flounder is Pleuronectes flesus. There are several common American species used as food; as the smooth flounder (P. glabra); the rough or winter flounder (P. Americanus); the summer flounder, or plaice (Paralichthys dentatus), Atlantic coast; and the starry flounder (Pleuronectes stellatus).

defs: A tool used in crimping boot fronts.

records.ety: [Cf. D. flodderen to flap, splash through mire, E. flounce, v.i., and flounder the fish.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Floundered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Floundering.]

defs: To fling the limbs and body, as in making efforts to move; to struggle, as a horse in the mire, or as a fish on land; to roll, toss, and tumble; to flounce.
quotes: They have floundered on from blunder to blunder.
